limitations: Normal event with no physical effect and zero anomaly labels. Operator evidence would be stable BESS power, SOC, and PCC power. Limitation: synthetic baseline variation only.

Plausible bounded BESS dispatch deviation with event-level control context. Model difficulty is moderate because the ramp is small and SOC evidence is slow. Limitation: SOC response is compiler-bounded.
Cyber-only event has no physical residuals. Operator evidence is delayed event-level reporting with stable physical values. Model difficulty is high for physical-only detectors.

Physical-only PV mismatch is plausible when PV output changes while irradiance remains contextual. Model difficulty is moderate because PV and PCC power move together. Limitation: no cyber anomaly is claimed.

Cyber-only suppressed acknowledgement leaves physical variables unchanged. Operator evidence is event-level delivery status mismatch. Model difficulty is high for a detector using only physical windows.

Bounded physical-only PCC voltage deviation is plausible and remains within voltage limits. Model difficulty is moderate because the magnitude is subtle across phases. Limitation: no cyber cause is asserted.
Plausible coordinated PV and BESS behavior with bounded net PCC effect. Model difficulty is high because compensation can hide aggregate power change. Operator evidence is DER-level mismatch.

Cyber-only availability context creates no physical residuals. Operator evidence is the event-level security category. Model difficulty is high for frozen physical detectors. Limitation: context-only label.

Bounded BESS reactive oscillation is plausible and should remain inside power limits. Model difficulty is moderate because periodic shape differs from normal ramps. Operator evidence is Q oscillation.
Irradiance is modified only in the allowed irradiance-like family, while temperature is not modified. Model difficulty is lower because irradiance and PV power are aligned.
Physical-only PCC current imbalance is bounded and does not change frequency or temperature. Model difficulty is moderate because only part of the three-phase pattern changes.

Plausible cyber-physical delay with a late but bounded physical response. Model difficulty is high because response timing, not magnitude alone, carries evidence. Limitation: no persistent delay after window.
Cyber-only integrity context intentionally produces no physical residual. Operator evidence is event-level integrity classification. Model difficulty is high for physical-only detection.
Coordinated PV-BESS reactive shift is bounded and plausible. Model difficulty is high because opposing DER responses reduce aggregate evidence. Operator evidence is asset-level Q mismatch.
Normal scenario has no physical residual and all labels are zero. Operator evidence is consistent PV, BESS, PCC, and irradiance behavior. Limitation: synthetic normal control variation.
Plausible SOC-aware BESS mismatch with bounded power and SOC effects. Model difficulty is moderate because SOC evolves slowly. Operator evidence is BESS power direction plus SOC trend.
Uses only final dataset PV/PCC variables and creates bounded physical residual inside the event window during morning ramp-up period.
Creates subtle stale measurement condition where SOC appears frozen while BESS continues operating; stays within 5-95% bounds.
Represents suppressed volt-var control command creating reactive power deviation; bounded within PV reactive limits.
Midday curtailment creates detectable mismatch between irradiance and actual PV output; uses percentage-based effect.
Creates single-phase stale voltage measurement while other phases update normally; subtle cross-phase inconsistency.
Represents delayed control response with extended ramp period; stays within BESS power bounds.

Cyber-only blocked command with no physical effect; tests cyber context detection without physical residuals.

Physical-only PCC voltage deviation with unbalanced sag across phases; stays within voltage bounds.
Physical-only BESS oscillation representing control instability; bounded oscillation within power limits.

Normal operational control scenario with no physical residual or anomaly labels; represents baseline behavior.
Normal operational scenario during evening irradiance decline; no physical residual or anomaly labels.
Normal bounded control variation with no anomaly labels or physical residuals. Uses only approved final dataset assets and features.
